meta.title: Abashiri Lake View Ski Area — LP Mock (Okhotsk Panorama · Drift Ice · Dual Pricing Strategy)
meta.description: 360° views of drift ice, Lake Abashiri, and the Sea of Okhotsk from Mt. Tento. Six courses, 900 m pair lift, night skiing until 21:00. Local day ticket ¥2,940 and 2-hour ¥1,200 held steady; Winter Iron Activity Pass ¥8,000–10,000; Night Snow Lounge ¥3,000; Okhotsk seafood bowls and year-round Panorama Cafe. Operated by Nissenren Okhotsk Abashiri · 428 free parking spaces. Evaluation mock based on strategy report.

banner.text: Drift-ice panorama · Winter Iron Pass · Night Snow Lounge · local pricing protected | AI-generated mock images

hero.title:
Abashiri Lake View
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Drift ice, Lake Abashiri, and the Sea of Okhotsk—a northern panorama from six courses and 900 m of lift.

transit.title:
Drift ice on the lake,
the sea from Mt. Tento.
transit.lead: Lake Abashiri and the Sea of Okhotsk—drift ice up close in winter—from a 360° vantage on Mt. Tento. Six courses, a 900 m pair lift, and night skiing until 21:00. Reachable by bus or rental car from Abashiri Station and Memanbetsu Airport—a base for Okhotsk stays tied to drift-ice sightseeing, the museum Big Three, and Tsuruga lakeside resort.
transit.imgAlt: Drift ice, Lake Abashiri, and the Sea of Okhotsk from Mt. Tento—a 360° panorama ski hill

primary.title: Drift ice · Lake Abashiri · Okhotsk Sea panorama
primary.lead: Six courses over 900 m from Mt. Tento—winter views of drift ice on Lake Abashiri and the Sea of Okhotsk. A 900 m pair lift and night skiing until 21:00. A local hill for Okhotsk culture and wide-open vistas, not a mega-resort circuit.
primary.imgAlt: Drift ice and Lake Abashiri from near Mt. Tento summit—a 360° Okhotsk panorama

secondary.title: A snowboard debut destination
secondary.lead: Gentle pitches and uncrowded six-course layout—ideal for a first snowboard day. Rental ¥1,000/hour, with hands-free bundles folded into the Winter Iron Pass. A "first snow in the north" base for families and couples (LP concept).

pass.title:
Local ¥2,940/day,
¥1,200 for 2 hours—unchanged.
pass.lead: Keep the local full-day ticket at ¥2,940 and the 2-hour ticket at ¥1,200 for Abashiri residents and neighbors—protecting everyday local use comes first. Revenue from distant and inbound visitors extends through packages such as the Winter Iron Activity Pass and Night Snow Lounge—a dual-pricing model (LP strategy).
pass.visitorNote: Visitor revenue designed through package products
pass.noticeBody: Do not raise local prices—capture visitor ARPU through the Winter Iron Pass (¥8,000–10,000) and Night Snow Lounge (¥3,000) bundled with drift ice, wakasagi fishing, and the museum Big Three—a revenue pivot for an Okhotsk panorama ski hill.
pass.imgAlt: Abashiri Lake View with local pricing protected—visitors deepen the experience through packages

package.title:
Winter Iron Activity Pass,
¥8,000–10,000.
package.lead: Morning wakasagi ice fishing on Lake Abashiri + 2 hours of skiing + hands-free rental—three Okhotsk winter staples on one pass. Safely bundles a drift-ice-season classic with the hill, extending stay length for beginner–intermediate and snowboard-debut guests (LP strategy).
package.whisper: Leave local pricing untouched—productize the visitor's "Okhotsk day." Wakasagi × ski is the differentiation core.
package.noticeBody: Wakasagi fishing is a Lake Abashiri staple—finish in the morning, then non-stop through 2 hours on snow and rental return. The backbone of a half-day to full-day model for families and inbound guests.
package.imgAlt: Wakasagi ice fishing on Lake Abashiri plus 2 hours on the hill—Winter Iron Activity Pass

dining.title:
Okhotsk seafood bowls,
beyond 11:00–14:00.
dining.lead: Hill restaurant reform—Okhotsk seafood rice bowls at ¥1,500–2,500 as the anchor, extending beyond the traditional short lunch window. A night food-truck café tied to night skiing and the year-round Okhotsk Panorama Cafe (hana-tento flower-garden asset) to grow reasons to stay beyond runs (LP concept).
dining.bowlLead: Hairy crab, scallops, salmon roe—the Okhotsk sea in a bowl. ¥1,500–2,500 as the lunch core and an après-ski staple.
dining.hoursLead: Expand from an 11:00–14:00 core into pre- and post–night-skiing service and weekend afternoons—serving both Night Snow Lounge guests and locals.
dining.foodTruckLead: Linked to 21:00 night skiing—hot drinks and light bites at the foot of the hill. An après zone where non-skiing companions are welcome.
dining.panoramaLead: Year-round café using the hana-tento flower-garden asset—sell the Mt. Tento panorama off-season too. An Abashiri stay base when drift ice is gone.
dining.noticeBody: Lift tickets alone are not enough—dining, café, and night food lift visitor ARPU while local pricing stays unchanged.
dining.imgAlt: Okhotsk seafood bowl—the flagship menu of hill restaurant reform

lounge.title:
Toast at the summit,
¥3,000.
lounge.lead: Night Snow Lounge—¥3,000 with Tsuruga resort shuttle. Non-skiing partners welcome: ride the lift to the summit for drinks above the snow. A night product linked to night skiing until 21:00—enjoyable even if you do not ski (LP strategy).
lounge.whisper: Solves "one skis, one doesn't" for couples and families—the summit lounge is the night differentiator and lodging link.
lounge.noticeBody: Shuttle for Tsuruga lakeside resort guests—also open to hill-only visitors, productizing Okhotsk nights.
lounge.imgAlt: Night Snow Lounge at the summit—Okhotsk night views and drinks under the lights

board.title:
First board day,
¥1,000/hour.
board.lead: Gentle six-course layout and low crowds—position Abashiri Lake View as a first-snowboard destination. Board rental ¥1,000/hour, with hands-free sets bundled in the Winter Iron Pass. An acquisition axis for families, couples, and inbound beginners (LP concept).
board.noticeBody: Not advanced-only—"debut plus panorama." Pair drift-ice views with a first Okhotsk snow experience.
board.imgAlt: First turns on gentle pitches—six debut-friendly courses at Abashiri Lake View

ecosystem.title:
Museum Big Three,
the lake and Tsuruga.
ecosystem.lead: Stays are not born on the hill alone—bundle Abashiri's cultural and natural assets with the Winter Iron Pass into a 1-night/2-day Okhotsk circuit (LP strategy).
ecosystem.prisonLead: National Important Cultural Property—experience a Meiji-era penal facility. An Abashiri winter staple and popular inbound history content.
ecosystem.driftLead: Science and hands-on drift-ice exhibits—the core of the Big Three, giving context to what you see from Mt. Tento.
ecosystem.peoplesLead: Ainu and Okhotsk cultures—life and art of northern peoples. Three museums together deepen an Abashiri stay.
ecosystem.wakasagiLead: Classic winter ice fishing—the morning slot on the Winter Iron Pass. Fry your catch at nearby shops.
ecosystem.tsurugaLead: Hot-spring resort on Lake Abashiri—Night Snow Lounge shuttle hub; lodging + pass + museums.
ecosystem.imgAlt: Okhotsk Drift Ice Museum—the cultural axis of the Big Three and Winter Iron Pass

journey.title:
From wakasagi
to the night lounge.
journey.lead: Morning wakasagi on Lake Abashiri, 2 hours on the Winter Iron Pass, museum Big Three in the afternoon, Night Snow Lounge from Tsuruga—a one-day Okhotsk model at ¥8,000–15,000 per visitor while local pricing stays protected (LP concept).
journey.badge: DRIFT ICE · LAKE ABASHIRI · OKHOTSK PANORAMA

01.body: Ice fishing experience—Winter Iron Pass morning slot

02.title: Afternoon 2-hour laps
02.body: 6 courses · 900 m · hands-free rental · pass for non-locals

04.title: Museum Big Three
04.body: Prison · drift ice · northern peoples—cultural afternoon

05.title: Tsuruga resort
05.body: Lakeside onsen · await Night Snow Lounge shuttle

panorama.title: Complete Mt. Tento panorama guide—drift ice, lake, and sea
panorama.body: Six courses, 900 m pair lift, night skiing to 21:00—360° views of drift ice, Lake Abashiri, and the Sea of Okhotsk from Mt. Tento. A local hill distinct from mega-resorts; 428 free parking spaces; operated by Nissenren Okhotsk Abashiri.

ironPass.title: Winter Iron Activity Pass—flagship product guide
ironPass.body: Morning wakasagi ice fishing + 2 hours of skiing + hands-free rental at ¥8,000–10,000—local tickets stay at ¥2,940/¥1,200; the visitor staple package. A half-day to full-day drift-ice-season model, also suited to snowboard debut guests (LP strategy).

ecosystem.title: Museum Big Three × Tsuruga × night lounge circuit
ecosystem.body: Museum Abashiri Prison, Okhotsk Drift Ice Museum, Northern Peoples Museum, Lake Abashiri wakasagi, Tsuruga lakeside resort, Night Snow Lounge ¥3,000, year-round Okhotsk Panorama Cafe—design a 1-night/2-day Okhotsk stay around the Winter Iron Pass.

access.note: Bus or taxi from Abashiri Station · about 40 km from Memanbetsu Airport · 428 free parking spaces
